title: loeselia glandulosa subsp. conglomerata
intro: Loeselia glandulosa subsp. conglomerata, also known as the glandular or conglomerate loeelia, is a plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) that is native to the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to induce sweating and promote the release of toxins, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as fever and rheumatism. The plant is rich in bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, offering potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In herbal preparations, Loeselia glandulosa subsp. conglomerata is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
